If you're in ultra-tight rapport, but you say something stupid, you break rapport and you can't close. If you have to run a really high level of certainty and you say things that don't end up, that certainty will drop. Certainty is not a constant. Repor is not a constant. Okay? And the fact is you're closing from the first word out of your mouth, not on buying from you. You're closing them if nothing more on listening to your next sentence, that you're a person worth listening to. As soon as you forget that and you think, okay, well, I'm in rapport, so now I can just, you know, move up. You can't. You'll fall out of rapport. The best metaphor I've ever seen for this is like when you watch political debates, much of a country arrange, we have people from all over the world, when you watch political debates, you notice like they'll have people in the audience with these thumb things, right? When a politician is like really lying, they're a tail off, he goes, sinks down to the ground. When they're saying something that resonates with the audience, it's honest, you'll see those, that's almost how it works when you're selling. Based on what you're saying, rapport is either going up or going down. It's never constant. Never. Certainty is not a constant. Repor is not a constant. And controlling the sale is not a constant. You don't just take control. You must maintain control, which is why you got to learn this at a deep level.
